description: initial IWM 60d plus10/minus5 drop-neutral baseline is the cleanest of the new macro basket and already passes the promotion gate on the first run
60d plus8/minus4 kept decent balance on IWM but did not beat the baseline on headline quality
60d plus12/minus6 made IWM cleaner but still failed to outperform the default 10 over 5 baseline
keep-all-binary reduced the need for neutral filtering on paper but still trailed the drop-neutral baseline on headline quality and test balance
rs_vs_benchmark_60 versus SPY is the strongest IWM feature add-on so far, but the default baseline still edges it out on headline score
IWM baseline still holds after rule and walk-forward validation, while rs_vs_benchmark_60 remains only a sidecar rather than a replacement

relative-strength plus trend-quality stack with ret_20_vs_benchmark price_ratio_benchmark_z_20 trend_quality_20 percent_up_days_20 bollinger_bandwidth_20 distance_from_60d_low and atr_pct_20_percentile finally beats the baseline on headline score while keeping test balance strong

relative-strength plus trend-quality stack beat the static split baseline, but threshold remained the only decent operating rule and 4-fold walk-forward still broke in the first two folds with test_bal_acc falling to 0.4491 and 0.5009, so it cannot replace the validated baseline yet

anti-overprediction threshold controls are now available repo-wide, but on IWM they did not rescue the context stack: caps and target-positive-rate penalties reduced early-fold positive rate without producing a cleaner walk-forward line, and the best neg-weight plus cap variants still left fold 1 and fold 2 below useful balanced-accuracy levels

structural gating on top of the IWM context stack found only a very sparse side candidate rather than a true breakthrough: `fixed_0.49 + above_200dma + ret_20_vs_benchmark>0` kept all three walk-forward folds near fifty-fifty balanced accuracy at 0.5025 0.5522 and 0.4978, but it produced only 8 total trades and the third fold still lost money

the cleaner second-stage IWM operator is now `fixed_0.49 + ret_20_vs_benchmark>0`: it stayed near break-even balanced accuracy across all three walk-forward folds at 0.5025 0.5931 and 0.4994 while lifting average fold return to 5.80 percent with 12 total trades, but the static split stayed far too sparse at only 4 test trades and 0.4948 balanced accuracy so it remains a selective side candidate rather than a full breakthrough

recent live-like comparison shows the `fixed_0.49 + ret_20_vs_benchmark>0` side operator is currently dormant rather than actionable: over the latest 60 saved test rows it selected 0 times while the validated baseline selected 41 times, and its full test-period activity was concentrated in only 14 dates ending on 2025-04-04
